meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to build high-converting user onboarding to overcome no design skills. A guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: You don't need a design degree to build professional onboarding. By leveraging UI component libraries and automated flow generation with Antigravity and Claude Code, you can implement functional, high-converting onboarding flows that guide users through your product, proving your technical competence while solving the visual design gap immediately.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding out backend logic, database schemas, and API endpoints. But when you open your browser, the interface stares back—a cold, uninviting void of unstyled HTML. For the student developer, this 'design paralysis' is the silent project killer. You aren't failing at code; you're just missing the bridge between functionality and user experience.

Great user onboarding is the ultimate force multiplier for your portfolio. It hides design simplicity behind sophisticated utility, turning a basic MVP into a polished, professional-grade application. With the right toolkit, you can transform your functional prototype into a guided journey that captivates users from the first click, regardless of your aesthetic background.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the three critical steps a user must take to reach their 'aha!' moment. Keep it lean to minimize UI complexity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a UX architect. Design a 3-step onboarding flow for a SaaS tool that focuses on minimal aesthetic requirements. Output a JSON schema for the user journey.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a journey_map.json based on the 3-step onboarding flow for a SaaS MVP'

steps.title: Bootstrap UI with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate clean, accessible components that don't rely on custom CSS, ensuring a professional look right out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React-based onboarding wizard component using utility-first CSS. Ensure accessibility compliance and clean, neutral styling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'generate onboarding-wizard.tsx using the Antigravity component library'

steps.title: Implement Progress State Management
steps.description: Track the user's progress through the onboarding. This provides visual feedback, which is key to UX even without complex design.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a state management hook for a multi-step onboarding wizard. Include local storage persistence to save user progress.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'implement useOnboardingState hook with local storage persistence'

steps.title: Automate Contextual Tooltips
steps.description: Use Claude Code to inject tooltips into your app. These act as 'guides' that mask the simplicity of your interface with helpful content.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a context-aware tooltip component that triggers on button focus. Keep the design minimal and professional.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'create Tooltip component and integrate into the main dashboard'

steps.title: Final Integration and QA
steps.description: Test the flow for friction points. Use Claude Code to identify where users might drop off and automate the fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the onboarding flow for potential UX friction points and suggest code-level improvements to reduce drop-off.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'run automated accessibility and flow check on the onboarding wizard'

pro_tips: Use a monochrome color palette; it looks 'minimalist' rather than 'unfinished'.
Prioritize 'Empty States' as much as onboarding; a clear prompt on a blank page is better than a broken UI.
Implement a 'skip' button; forcing users through onboarding is the fastest way to lose them.
Copy is your best design tool; use clear, action-oriented verbs instead of abstract labels.
Ship the onboarding as a separate module so you can refactor or replace it without breaking your core logic.
content.next_steps: Once the onboarding flow is live, focus on building a 'User Dashboard' that reflects the progress made during onboarding. This creates a cohesive narrative for your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You are closer than ever to launching a project that doesn't just work, but feels professional. Stop letting design anxiety stall your progress and start leveraging these AI tools to bridge the gap today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
